body: # print() sends text to the console — your first line of Python!
# The text inside quotes is called a "string"
# range(n) generates numbers from 0 to n-1
# The loop variable '${1:i}' takes each value in turn
For Loop.description: For loop with range and teaching comments

body: # A while loop repeats as long as its condition is True
# Make sure the condition eventually becomes False, or you get an infinite loop!
While Loop.description: While loop with counter and teaching comments

body: """${3:Brief description of what this function does.}
# 'self' refers to the instance being created
# try/except handles errors gracefully instead of crashing
${1:# Code that might raise an error}
# This runs if an error occurs in the try block
# This ALWAYS runs, error or not — good for cleanup
# List comprehension: a concise way to create lists
# Equivalent to a for loop that appends to a list
# Dict comprehension: create a dictionary in one line
# Format: {key: value for item in iterable}
# 'with' automatically closes the file when done — always use it!
File Read.description: Read a file with context manager

body: # 'w' means write mode — WARNING: this overwrites existing content!
# Use 'a' instead of 'w' to append without overwriting
File Write.description: Write to a file with context manager

body: # This block only runs when the script is executed directly,
# NOT when it's imported as a module by another script
# Import a module to use its functions and classes
# 'as' creates a shorter alias for convenience
"""Set up test fixtures — runs before each test method."""
# Arrange: set up the test data
# Act: call the code being tested
# Assert: verify the result matches expectations
Unit Test.description: Unit test class with setUp and test method

body: # argparse handles command-line arguments for your script
Argparse.description: Argparse template with positional and optional arguments

body: # enumerate() gives you both the index AND the value in a loop
# Much cleaner than manually tracking an index counter!
Enumerate.description: Enumerate loop with index and value

body: # zip() pairs up elements from two (or more) lists
# It stops at the shortest list's length
Zip Loop.description: Zip loop to iterate over parallel lists
